# LMDB Wrapper - MISRA C

> MISRA C compliant wrapper for LMDB (Lightning Memory-Mapped Database)

## Overview

A C99 library that provides a safe, validated API layer over LMDB enforcing MISRA C coding standards. Designed for safety-critical and embedded systems that need reliable key-value storage with strict error handling.

## Key Facts

- Language: C (C99 standard)
- License: MIT
- Build system: CMake >= 3.14
- Dependencies: LMDB (auto-downloaded by CMake)
- Testing: CMocka framework
- MISRA C compliant: full parameter validation, explicit error codes, single return point per function
- Available via [vcpkg](https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg): `vcpkg install ddtdanilo-lmdb-wrapper`

## API

The public API is declared in `include/lmdb_wrapper.h`. All functions use the `lmdbWrapper` prefix.

### Types

- `lmdbWrapperErr_t` — Error code enum returned by all functions
- `lmdbWrapperEnv_t` — Opaque environment handle
- `lmdbWrapperConfig_t` — Configuration struct with fields: `path` (const char*), `mapSize` (size_t), `maxDbs` (uint32_t)

### Functions

- `lmdbWrapperEnvCreate(lmdbWrapperEnv_t **envOut, const lmdbWrapperConfig_t *config)` — Create and open a database environment
- `lmdbWrapperEnvClose(lmdbWrapperEnv_t *env)` — Close and destroy an environment
- `lmdbWrapperPut(lmdbWrapperEnv_t *env, const void *key, size_t keySize, const void *val, size_t valSize)` — Store a key-value record
- `lmdbWrapperGet(lmdbWrapperEnv_t *env, const void *key, size_t keySize, void *valBuf, size_t valCapacity, size_t *valSizeOut)` — Copy a value by key into a caller buffer
- `lmdbWrapperDel(lmdbWrapperEnv_t *env, const void *key, size_t keySize)` — Delete a record by key
- `lmdbWrapperStrerror(lmdbWrapperErr_t err)` — Convert error code to human-readable string

### Error Codes

- `LMDB_WRAPPER_SUCCESS` (0) — Operation succeeded
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_NULL_PARAM` (-1) — A required parameter was NULL
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_INVALID_PARAM` (-2) — A parameter had an invalid value
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_ENV_CREATE` (-3) — Failed to create LMDB environment
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_ENV_OPEN` (-4) — Failed to open LMDB environment
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_TXN_BEGIN` (-5) — Failed to begin transaction
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_DBI_OPEN` (-6) — Failed to open database handle
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_PUT` (-7) — Failed to write record
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_GET` (-8) — Failed to read record
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_DEL` (-9) — Failed to delete record
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_TXN_COMMIT` (-10) — Failed to commit transaction
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_NOT_FOUND` (-11) — Record not found
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_MAP_FULL` (-12) — Database map is full
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL` (-13) — Output buffer is too small
- `LMDB_WRAPPER_ERR_INTERNAL` (-99) — Unexpected internal error

## Use Cases

- Blockchain and cryptocurrency (e.g. Monero stores its blockchain in LMDB)
- Safety-critical embedded systems: automotive (AUTOSAR), medical (IEC 62304), industrial (IEC 61508)
- IoT and firmware: persistent config, structured logs, state on constrained devices
- Infrastructure: OpenLDAP, Postfix, session/cache storage
- AI/ML pipelines: Caffe/Caffe2 use LMDB for image dataset storage
- Desktop/mobile apps: lightweight key-value cache alternative to SQLite

This MISRA C wrapper adds the mandatory safety layer (parameter validation, explicit error codes, single return point) required in regulated and safety-critical environments.

## Usage Example

```c
#include "lmdb_wrapper.h"
#include <string.h>

int main(void) {
    lmdbWrapperEnv_t *env = NULL;
    lmdbWrapperConfig_t config = {
        .path = "./my_database",
        .mapSize = 0,  /* default 10MB */
        .maxDbs = 0    /* default 1 */
    };

    lmdbWrapperErr_t err = lmdbWrapperEnvCreate(&env, &config);
    if (err != LMDB_WRAPPER_SUCCESS) {
        return 1;
    }

    const char *key = "block_001";
    const char *val = "data...";
    err = lmdbWrapperPut(env, key, strlen(key), val, strlen(val));

    char readVal[64] = {0};
    size_t readSize = 0;
    err = lmdbWrapperGet(env, key, strlen(key),
                         readVal, sizeof(readVal), &readSize);

    err = lmdbWrapperDel(env, key, strlen(key));

    lmdbWrapperEnvClose(env);
    return 0;
}
```

## Installation

### vcpkg (recommended)

```bash
vcpkg install ddtdanilo-lmdb-wrapper
```

CMake integration:

```cmake
find_package(lmdb_wrapper CONFIG REQUIRED)
target_link_libraries(your_target PRIVATE lmdb_wrapper::lmdb_wrapper)
```

### Build from source

```bash
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug
cmake --build build
cd build && ctest --output-on-failure
```

## File Structure

- `include/lmdb_wrapper.h` — Public API header
- `src/lmdb_wrapper.c` — Implementation
- `tests/test_lmdb_wrapper.c` — Test suite (CMocka)
- `examples/basic_usage.c` — Complete usage example
- `cmake/FetchLMDB.cmake` — Auto-downloads LMDB dependency
- `vcpkg-port/` — Reference copy of vcpkg port files (`ddtdanilo-lmdb-wrapper`)
- `docs/` — Architecture and context documentation

## Links

- Source: https://github.com/ddtdanilo/LMDB-wrapper-MISRA-C
- Full docs: https://github.com/ddtdanilo/LMDB-wrapper-MISRA-C/blob/main/llms-full.txt
